subject: UX & Research
title: Lighthouse and Automated Testing
description: Learn Lighthouse and Automated Testing in one minute.

hook.text: Run Lighthouse. Score: 98. Ship it? Not so fast. Automated tools catch only about 30% of real issues.

buildup.text: Lighthouse checks alt text, contrast, ARIA labels, and heading structure. It's fast and free in Chrome.

discovery.text: axe-core and WAVE are popular alternatives. Each tool catches slightly different issues. Use multiple.

twist.text: A score of 100 doesn't mean accessible. It means you passed automated checks. Manual testing is still needed.

climax.text: Use automated tools for fast first-pass screening. Then test with real assistive technology users.

punchline.text: Automated tools are step one. Manual testing completes the picture.

quiz.question: Why is a perfect Lighthouse accessibility score insufficient?
options: Automated tools only catch about 30% of real accessibility issues — manual testing is still needed
Perfect scores mean the site needs no further testing
